basics.name: KESHAV RAO
basics.title: Software Developer & AI/ML Engineer
basics.summary: AI/ML Engineer and Software Developer specializing in Generative AI, RAG microservices, MCP orchestration, and scalable cloud architectures. Proven track record in developing high-accuracy predictive models and optimizing LLM workflows.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
bullets: Relevant coursework: Artificial Intelligence, Natural Language Processing, Algorithms, Machine Learning, Distributed Systems

education.institution: SRM Institute of Science and Technology
education.degree: Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering

bullets: Part of the Gameplay Analyzer team where we take video footage of older gameplay and try to better understand logic to train our AI model.
Building logic for NPCs in an american football game that is currently in development.
Built and deployed cloud-hosted SaaS web applications and backend APIs using IBM watsonx Assistant, implementing intent classification, slot-filling, and multi-turn conversational workflows.
Optimized responses from foundation models (Granite, Llama-2, Mistral) using prompt engineering, temperature tuning, and decoding strategies.
Built API orchestration workflows integrating backend services and microservices into multi-turn conversational systems.
Shipped React/TypeScript UI and Node.js REST APIs powering LLM customer support agents to production for enterprise clients, serving ~1K users via IBM watsonx.
Improved response quality by ~30% across Granite, Llama-2, and Mistral model families through structured prompt design and decoding optimization.
Optimized end-to-end inference latency by ~15% through model selection tuning and response caching, enabling real-time agent responses at production scale.
Designed AI-based resource allocation algorithms using Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) and Iterative Deepening DFS, reducing scheduling latency by 20% in edge-cloud simulations.
Improved scalability of service function chaining by integrating disjoint-set union-find structures within the VAPNIC framework across 50+ distributed nodes.
Built a modular edge-cloud orchestration system supporting multiple schedulers and advanced data structures (Van Emde Boas trees), improving simulation efficiency by 25%.
Designed and implemented scheduling algorithms for compute tasks across edge and cloud infrastructure, improving distributed system throughput and scalability.

experience.company: SRM Institute of Science and Technology
bullets: Built machine learning models (Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost) for coronary artery disease prediction using 1,000+ patient records, achieving 98% classification accuracy.
Reduced required diagnostic tests by 50% by identifying key predictive features through feature importance analysis.
Engineered modular, reusable frontend architecture using React.js and TypeScript, emphasizing scalability and maintainability.
Designed and integrated RESTful services with structured API configuration, request lifecycle management, and robust error handling.
Implemented efficient state management and optimized asynchronous data flows to improve reliability of frontend-backend interactions.

achievements.context: Classification accuracy achieved in ML models for coronary artery disease prediction using 1,000+ patient records.
Reduction in required diagnostic tests by identifying key predictive features through feature importance analysis.
Improvement in simulation efficiency by building a modular edge-cloud orchestration system.
Reduction in scheduling latency in edge-cloud simulations using MCTS and Iterative Deepening DFS.
Average accuracy (92% peak) achieved in a machine learning pipeline for phishing webpage detection.

projects.title: AI Knowledge Platform (RAG Microservices + MCP)
bullets: Developed microservice-based REST APIs for web applications enabling natural language search over enterprise document repositories using Elasticsearch semantic retrieval.
Designed server-side retrieval workflows and backend services integrating Claude via MCP server orchestration for contextaware responses.
Built a React-based interactive web application integrating Stable Diffusion XL to generate marketing assets from datasets.
Implemented TypeScript/Node.js middleware using Elasticsearch as a dual-purpose vector and keyword search engine with embedding, chunking, reranking, and MCP-based model calls.
Achieved <1.5s retrieval latency and reduced hallucinations through retrieval evaluation and iterative tuning of chunk size, overlap, and prompt templates.

projects.title: AI Automation Platform for Generative Content
projects.stack: Python, PyTorch, Stable Diffusion XL, React
bullets: Built an AI automation platform integrating Stable Diffusion XL with a React interface with generation of marketing assets from structured datasets.
Developed backend inference pipelines and server-side processing services using PyTorch and HuggingFace Diffusers with persistent model caching.
Designed batch generation workflows enabling scalable asset creation from client data.
Built an end-to-end pipeline to generate personalized recruiter outreach emails and marketing assets at scale, reducing asset creation time by 20%.

projects.title: Phishing Webpage Detection System
bullets: Developed a machine learning pipeline for phishing webpage detection, achieving 90.75% average accuracy (92% peak).
Improved model performance by 3.5% using hyperparameter tuning, early stopping, and learning-rate scheduling.
Built a React and TypeScript interactive web dashboard to visualize model predictions and training metrics.
Implemented real-time prediction visualization through REST API integration.

certifications: The Complete React Developer Course (w/ Hooks and Redux)
Perform Foundational Infrastructure Tasks in Google Cloud
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Foundations 2021 Certified Associate

extra.content: B. Balakrishnan, K. Kumaran, A. Sankar and K. Rao, "Coronary Artery Disease Prediction using Machine Learning Algorithms," 2022 pp. 95-100, doi: 10.1109/ICESIC53714.2022.9783592
